component.name: Kopilka
component.summary: Couples budget planner with shared finance management

description.p: Kopilka is a GTK4 budget planner for two-person households. Track income, fixed expenses, debt, discretionary spending, assets, and savings goals in a shared JSON file that can be synced via pCloud or any shared folder.

ul.li: Dashboard with income breakdown, net budget, bills calendar, net worth, and category spending bars
Income tracking for multiple sources per person
Fixed expenses with calendar view and next-due dates
Debt management with payoff projections and what-if extra payment slider
Discretionary spending with per-category budgets, rollover, and seasonal overrides
One-time purchase pool for irregular expenses
Recurring entries that auto-insert into the spending log
Spending log grouped by date with category tagging
Reports with donut charts, monthly bar charts, and per-category trends
Asset accounts with balance history and savings goal tracker
Sync via pCloud — detects external file changes on window focus and offers a one-click reload

screenshot.caption: Dashboard showing income, budget breakdown, and upcoming bills

description.p: "Level Ledger" — math corrections, crash fixes, and UX clarity improvements.

ul.li: Annual one-time purchase pool now correctly uses unallocated discretionary funds, not the full pre-category discretionary amount.
Debt avalanche and snowball strategies now model payment cascade — freed payments from a paid-off debt roll into the next priority debt.
Recurring entries no longer risk crashing the app at startup; processing is now owned by the main window after all views are initialised, and also fires when the window regains focus.
Budget saves are now atomic (temp-file + rename) — a crash during write can no longer corrupt budget.json.
Category color pills with light backgrounds (yellow, stone) now use black text for legibility.
CSV export from the spending log now respects the active time filter instead of always exporting full history.
One-time date fields in income and expense forms now validate the ISO date before saving.
Monthly Reports summary now applies seasonal budget overrides for the current month.
Monthly trend bars now show category spending only; fixed one-time expenses are annotated separately.
Dashboard category rows now display the time window (e.g. "week of 16 Jun") so mixed weekly/monthly rows are unambiguous.
Bill reminders now show billing frequency in the subtitle and per-period unit on the amount.
Income rows now display the per-period unit (e.g. $3,500/2wk).
Income amount field now prompts to enter take-home (after-tax) pay.

description.p: Bug fixes: calculations, data integrity, and feedback.

ul.li: Biweekly income and expense amounts now use the correct monthly conversion factor (26/12 instead of 2.165).
Recurring entries with a past next_date no longer freeze the app on open.
Monthly budget category overrides are no longer silently lost when the budget file is reloaded.
A toast is shown if the budget file cannot be saved (disk full, permissions error).
Deleting a category now shows how many spending entries will become "Unknown category".
Version number in the status bar and About dialog is now consistent.
App now opens correctly from the GNOME launcher and Discover — repeated launches raise the existing window instead of failing silently.
Fixed a GTK markup warning caused by a bare ampersand in the "Savings & Investments" dashboard label.

description.p: Status bar with toggles and about dialog.

ul.li: New status bar at the bottom of the window with SIMPLE and GOST toggle buttons — replaces the sidebar switches.
Active toggles appear bold; inactive toggles are dimmed — consistent with Rubric and Zerkalo.
Version chip on the right opens the GNOME About dialog with release notes.

description.p: Critical fix: app now opens correctly on all Linux distributions.

ul.li: Flatpak was built against GNOME Platform 50, which is not yet available on most systems — downgraded to Platform 48.
Desktop file missing StartupWMClass — launcher icon and window now group correctly in the taskbar.
Settings view showed stale partner names and bills look-ahead after a budget reload.
Budget open/create errors now show a toast instead of silently failing.
Recurring entry insertion no longer causes the spending log to render twice.

description.p: Delete confirmation dialogs, Today button on all date fields, friendly date headers in spending log, budget load error notification, and several bug fixes.
